script:
Hello, this is The Portfolio Podcast for MEME. Wednesday — here is your MEME recap. The portfolio closed down two point four three percent on the session. It was a broad-based decline, with eight of nine primary holdings finishing in the red.

Applied Optoelectronics led the damage. A fresh Sell rating dropped Wednesday morning, and the stock followed. Down seven point three three percent on a single session, the largest holding in this portfolio is now the most urgent question on the board.

No macro data was available for Wednesday's session. Markets appeared to trade on individual company news and sector rotation rather than a single macro catalyst. Broad market context remains limited, but technology names broadly faced pressure consistent with the portfolio's aggregate loss.

Applied Optoelectronics fell seven point three three percent Wednesday, making it the single largest drag on the portfolio. The move was catalyzed by a Sell rating published on Seeking Alpha at approximately one o'clock Eastern, with a bearish sentiment score of zero point two four. The analyst's core argument: despite AI-driven revenue growth, the business runs on gross margins around thirty percent, well below what the current valuation implies. Persistent operating losses, ongoing share dilution, and concentration risk around a small number of key customers were cited as structural headwinds. At seven point four three percent of this portfolio, Applied Optoelectronics carries more weight than any other position. A seven-plus percent single-session decline against that backdrop is material. The key question going forward is whether AI data center demand is sufficient to justify the multiple — and Wednesday's market said it is not, at least for now.

Turning to Redwire Corporation, down four point five six percent on the session. Two separate pieces of coverage circulated Wednesday. A Yahoo Finance comparison piece pitted Redwire directly against AST SpaceMobile in the space systems economy, and a separate article questioned whether drone-adjacent names including Redwire are running out of fuel following Red Cat's twenty-six percent decline over the past month. Neither piece was explicitly bullish. Watch for any contract or program announcements that can separate Redwire's fundamentals from sector sentiment.

AST SpaceMobile was down two point one nine percent. Two notable pieces hit Wednesday. A morning article highlighted the company's recent debt financing, framing one billion dollars in new debt as a catalyst that could fund satellite deployment. A second piece argued AST SpaceMobile is a more compelling space play than SpaceX on a pure risk-reward basis. Despite those constructive narratives, the stock finished lower, suggesting broader selling pressure in the sector outweighed the bullish case. Satellite deployment timelines remain the central watchpoint.

Nebius Group finished up zero point five seven percent, one of only two primary holdings to close in the green Wednesday. Baird launched bullish coverage on Nebius alongside CoreWeave, citing strong AI infrastructure demand. That call drove both names higher intraday, according to reporting from Yahoo Finance and finance.yahoo.com at four thirteen Eastern. Nebius carries six point zero one percent weight here, and analyst coverage initiations at this stage of a growth cycle deserve attention.

Bloom Energy fell three point five five percent. A Polen Capital investor letter highlighted the stock as a beneficiary of AI-driven power demand, with the firm's small-mid growth strategy returning twenty-eight point four percent gross in the second quarter. That bullish framing carried a sentiment score of zero point three nine. A separate article cautioned that some beaten-down tech names may not bounce the way pandemic-era declines did. Bloom Energy sat at the intersection of both narratives Wednesday, finishing lower despite the constructive institutional commentary.

TeraWulf was down one point nine one percent. No company-specific news circulated Wednesday. The move is consistent with broader pressure on Bitcoin mining and AI compute infrastructure names. Any shift in Bitcoin price or network hash rate will be the near-term driver to monitor.

Lumentum Holdings declined zero point nine four percent, the softest loss among the declining names. After the close, a Yahoo Finance piece flagged a Barclays upgrade to Overweight and earnings beat expectations, noting a one-day return of nine point four one percent at some prior session and a year-to-date return of one hundred sixteen point nine two percent. That post-close coverage could set up a different tone for Thursday. Watch the earnings date closely as it approaches.

IREN Limited was essentially flat, down zero point zero two percent. No company-specific news was available. The negligible move against a broadly negative tape could be read as relative stability. Power costs and Bitcoin price remain the key fundamental inputs.

Sandisk Corporation closed up zero point six two percent, the second green name in the portfolio Wednesday. The stock appeared in a broader market recap noting Super Micro Computer's nineteen point eight percent surge on upgraded gross margin guidance. Sandisk did not drive that narrative but traded in sympathy with positive storage and compute sentiment.

Three data points to watch in the next forty-eight hours. First, any response or guidance update from Applied Optoelectronics following Wednesday's Sell rating — management silence will be noted. Second, Lumentum Holdings' upcoming earnings date, flagged explicitly in post-close analyst coverage as a near-term catalyst. Third, any Federal Reserve speaker commentary that could reset discount rate expectations for high-growth, low-margin technology names broadly represented in this portfolio.

This is The Portfolio Podcast for MEME. We will be back same time the next business day with tomorrow's news. Watch Applied Optoelectronics closely — how management and the market respond to that Sell rating will set the tone heading into the back half of the week.
